content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Robert D. Blasier, Jr.
Date: Jan. 8, 1982

RE: INTERVIEW:

TELEPHONE (X) OTHER ( )

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Judy Davis

Subject: Opinion on Late Filing Fines

PERSON(S) INTERVIEWED:
Phyllis Johnson OF Registrar of Voters
TELEPHONE NO. (714)5419 or 2269 ADDRESS Orange County

DETAILS:

On January 5, 1982, I received a call from Phyllis Johnson, Registrar of Voters, Orange County, (714) 5419 or 2269.

Ms. Johnson stated that a successful judicial candidate had been assessed approximately $600 in late filing fines. As he refused to pay, the matter was turned over to the Orange County District Attorney, who filed suit in Municipal Court. Apparently, during his court appearance, the judge did not contest the amount of the fine, but argued that under Section 91013, his treasurer, and not he, should be liable for the fine, as the treasurer filed the campaign reports late. Ms. Johnson wanted an opinion as to the correctness of the judge's opinion.

After consulting Bob Blasier, I informed Ms. Johnson that under the Act, candidates are responsible for filing statements timely where they are filing a candidate's statement; treasurers are responsible where a committee statement is being filed; and where, as here, a consolidated campaign statement was filed, both candidate and treasurer are responsible and both jointly and severally liable for the full amount of the fine.

JD:er
